channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: British Nationals Return Home on First Government Flight from Middle East Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: British nationals have recounted their experiences travelling back to the UK on the first government-chartered flight from the Middle East since regional conflict escalated six days ago. Originally sc...

item.title: Rep. Tony Gonzales to Complete Current Term but Will Not Run for Re-election Following Ethics Probe
item.description: Rep. Tony Gonzales, a Republican from Texas, announced that he will not seek re-election following revelations of an extramarital affair with a former staffer and an ensuing House Ethics Committee inv...

item.title: Sauti za Busara Festival in Zanzibar Invites African Artists to Apply for 2027 Edition
item.description: The organisers of Sauti za Busara, one of the biggest music festivals in East Africa, have started accepting applications for musicians to perform at the 2027 edition. This popular event will happen i...

item.title: Legal and Political Dispute Over Linda Mwananchi Party Name in Kenya
item.description: The Linda Mwananchi Party of Kenya is at the centre of a heated legal and political dispute. Charles Wanyonyi, who applied to reserve the party name, is challenging objections from opposition leaders ...

item.title: Understanding the Role of Housing for Women-Headed Households in South Africa
item.description: Since 1994, South Africa has struggled to meet the demand for adequate housing, with the current national shortfall standing at around 2 million units despite delivering over 5 million homes and housi...

item.title: Iran Faces Conflict Alone as Global Powers Refrain from Military Support
item.description: With the assassination of its supreme leader and increasing military pressure from the United States and Israel, Iran is now largely isolated on the world stage. Russia and China, Tehran’s traditional...

item.title: Commercial flights bring Australians home from the Middle East amid safety concerns
item.description: Commercial flights carrying Australians from the Middle East are arriving with empty seats due to last-minute confirmations from airlines and concerns about airspace safety following recent US-Israeli...

item.title: Israeli Government Faces Lawsuits from Contractors and Influencers Over Unpaid Propaganda Work During Gaza Conflict
item.description: Israel's international propaganda apparatus, managed through the Prime Minister's Office after the collapse of the Ministry of Information following the October 2023 events, is encountering significan...

item.title: Stakeholders Ask NSC to Hire Multilingual Staff to Improve Communication at Border Posts
item.description: The National Association of Chambers of Commerce, Industry, Mines and Agriculture (NACCIMA) don raise alarm say language wahala dey affect cross-border trade for Nigeria, especially for traders wey de...

item.title: A Practical Guide to Selecting a Home That Fits Your Daily Life
item.description: Choosing a home is one of the most significant decisions a person can make, both financially and personally. The process should go beyond visual appeal or price, focusing instead on how the property a...

item.title: How land use and tourism are shaping lion populations in the Mara
item.description: The Maasai Mara is facing a subtle but serious challenge as lions are increasingly being displaced due to human activity. Research conducted by Kenya Wildlife Trust (KWT) between 2014 and 2023 across ...

item.title: Dar es Salaam Launches Waste-to-Energy Plan to Fight Climate Change
item.description: Dar es Salaam region has officially started a new programme to fight climate change by turning waste into useful resources, creating jobs, and producing clean energy. The initiative, led by the Dar es...

item.title: Quezon City Rep. Bong Suntay faces Ombudsman complaint over remarks about Anne Curtis
item.description: Quezon City Representative Bong Suntay is facing a complaint filed with the Office of the Ombudsman regarding his comments about actress Anne Curtis. Nine complainants, including members of the World ...

item.title: Pakistani Currency Traders Attempt to Resume Transactions with Dubai Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: KARACHI: Due to the recent US-Israel attack on Iran, Dubai has become a conflict zone, disrupting the usual trade and financial linkages with Pakistan. Currency traders in Pakistan are now trying to r...

item.title: Nitish Kumar resigns as Bihar Chief Minister and prepares for Rajya Sabha membership
item.description: The news reports that Nitish Kumar has resigned from his post as the Chief Minister of Bihar. This move indicates a shift in his political career as he prepares to take a seat in the Rajya Sabha. Over...

item.title: NSC Assures Public: Leaked Data from Alleged Chinese Spies Not a Major Security Threat
item.description: The National Security Council (NSC) clarified that recent reports regarding leaked information allegedly obtained by Chinese spies do not present a serious threat to the country. According to NSC offi...

item.title: Teachers in Kenya Now Enjoy Uninterrupted Healthcare Through POMSF
item.description: The Kenyan Ministry of Health has reassured teachers and the general public that the Public Officers Medical Scheme Fund (POMSF) is fully functional, offering continuous healthcare services for educat...

item.title: Manyaku Mashilo Explores Ancestry and Spirituality Through Art
item.description: Cape Town-based artist Manyaku Mashilo delves into ancestry, spirituality, and community memory through her evocative mixed-media works. Born in Limpopo in 1991, Mashilo constructs layered paintings, ...

item.title: Kurds Prepare for Possible Involvement in Iran Conflict Amid U.S. Support
item.description: Thousands of experienced Kurdish fighters in northern Iraq are reportedly preparing for possible military operations across the border in Iran, potentially with U.S. backing. Kurdish officials indicat...

item.title: Matiang'i Urges Strong Measures Against Corruption and Advocates for Youth Inclusion
item.description: During a lecture at Chatham House in the UK, Kenyan presidential hopeful Fred Matiang'i urged the country to intensify efforts against corruption and emphasised the need for meritocracy in public serv...
